results.title: Occidental Petroleum AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Occidental Petroleum (OXY) has emerged as a leader in AI-driven energy operations under the leadership of Patrick Bangert, VP and Chief of AI, who joined in August 2024[1]. The company focuses on practical AI implementation across its operations, emphasizing execution over technology hype. OXY has deployed AI for predictive maintenance across drilling rigs, production facilities, and operational monitoring[2]. In December 2025, OXY was featured at the Energy LIVE conference highlighting its advanced analytics and AI capabilities through presentations by Chief of AI Patrick Bangert and Manager of Advanced Analytics Dipti Sankpal[3]. The company positions itself as leveraging AI to enhance operational efficiency, reduce costs, and optimize production while supporting its transition to lower-carbon operations through direct air capture and carbon management technologies[4][5].

results.title: Advisor IT Systems | Hirequorum
highlights:
6

Oxy produces, markets and transports oil and natural gas to maximize value and provide resources fundamental to life. The company leverages its global leadership in carbon management to advance lower-carbon technologies and products. Headquartered in Houston, Oxy primarily operates in the United States, Middle East and North Africa. To learn more, visit Oxy Oxy strives to attract and retain talented employees by investing in their professional development and providing rewarding opportunities for personal growth. Our goal is to meet the highest employer standards by ensuring the health and safety of our employees, protecting the environment and positively impacting our communities where we do business. We are seeking a mid‑career MLOps / AI Ops Engineer to support the deployment, monitoring, and lifecycle management of machine learning and advanced analytics solutions across upstream Oil & Gas operations. This role bridges data science, cloud engineering, and operations to ensure reliable, scalable, and secure AI systems in production. Key Responsibilities Design, build, and maintain MLOps pipelines and platforms for model training, deployment, monitoring, and retraining using AWS. Operationalize ML models for upstream use cases (e.g., production optimization, subsurface modeling, drilling analytics). Implement CI/CD, model versioning, experiment tracking, and performance monitoring. Collaborate with data scientists, data engineers, and domain experts to move models from development to production. Ensure reliability, observability, governance, and compliance of ML systems. Troubleshoot production issues related to data, models, and infrastructure. Required Qualifications 5+ years of experience in data engineering, software engineering, MLOps, or AI Ops. Good grasp of software architecture principles and systems design Strong proficiency in Python for production‑grade ML workflows. Hands‑on experience with AWS (e.g., S3, EC2, EKS/ECS, SageMaker, Lambda, CloudWatch). Experience deploying and supporting ML models in production environments. Familiarity with CI/CD tools, Docker, and Kubernetes. Understanding of ML lifecycle management, model monitoring, and data drift. Preferred Qualifications Experience supporting analytics or ML solutions in upstream Oil & Gas or energy. Knowledge of time‑series, forecasting, or physics‑informed ML workloads. Experience with infrastructure‑as‑code (Terraform, CloudFormation). Recruitment Fraud It has come to our attention various individuals and/or organizations are contacting people falsely pretending to recruit on behalf of Oxy. Please be aware that these recruiting scams and communications do not originate nor are they associated with our recruitment process. All Oxy job postings and offers will require a completed application through our company website. Oxy does not charge a fee at any stage of the recruiting process. We will never: • Ask you to pay for applications, interviews, meetings, processing, training or for any other fees • Use recruiting or placement agencies that charge candidates an advance fee of any kind or • Request personal information such as passport and bank account details at an early stage of our recruitment process. We recommend against responding to unsolicited business propositions or offers from people you don't know. Do not disclose your personal or financial details. If you believe you have been the victim of a recruiting scam, please contact your local police department. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, disability status, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, citizenship status or any other basis as protected by federal, state, or local law. Oxy has bold ambitions to achieve net zero and help others do the same. To get there, we’ll need great minds ready to

results.title: Kenneth Igben
highlights:
Senior Data Advisor at Occidental Petroleum
...
Information System and Database Architectural Design and Administration, and Data Analytics, with a focus on integrating Geospatial Technology with Data Sciences and Machine Learning both On-Premise and Cloud Deployment.
...
Design and implement data architectures tailored for surface well planning, integrating business intelligence with big data to improve user productivity and system performance. Develop and deploy advanced process-management and software-integration technologies that generate, visualize, and maintain geometric relationships between facility well pads, surface intercepts, and pad extensions. Automate the creation of coordinate datasets for pre-staked wellbores and integrate them seamlessly into ESRI ArcGIS for clear visualization by regulators, planners, and schedulers. In parallel, also lead the development of engineering tools in C# and Python that optimize pad types, layouts, and wellbore spacing to streamline planning workflows. Leveraging the ESRI Geometry.Instance class in C#, I handle complex spatial data operations and automate the creation of essential geometric features ensuring precise, efficient, and scalable surface well planning.
...
Design data architecture specifically for surface well planning, integrating business intelligence with big data to enhance user productivity and performance. I develop and deploy advanced process management and software integration technologies aimed at generating, visualizing, and maintaining geometric relationships between facility well pads, surface intercepts, and pad extensions. Python scripts are utilized to generate coordinates for pre-staked wellbores, seamlessly integrating them with systems like ESRI ArcGIS for easy visualization by regulators, planners, and schedulers. Simultaneously, I lead the development of engineering tools using C# and Python, optimizing pad types and layouts to streamline planning and optimize wellbore spacing. Leverage the ESRI Geometry.Instance class in C# to handle complex spatial data operations and automate the generation of geometric data, creating points, polylines, and polygons essential for surface well planning. Applications I develop integrate with ESRI ArcGIS to visualize spatial relationships between facility well pads, surface intercepts, and pad extensions, ensuring accurate and efficient planning and wellbore spacing. Apply machine learning methodologies such as Random Forest, XGBoost, and Neural Networks to predict optimal well locations and develop predictive analytics tools for forecasting drilling outcomes and identifying potential risks. This improves project planning and enhances risk management strategies. By integrating big data analytics and machine learning techniques, I analyze large datasets to provide actionable insights for optimizing wellbore spacing, pad layouts, and drilling schedules. Real-time dashboards and reports generated through machine learning models facilitate data-driven decision-making, ensuring high performance and reliability in operational planning and execution.

results.title: Occidental Petroleum Software Engineer Interview Questions 2026
highlights:
Occidental Petroleum Software Engineer Interview Questions 2026
...
Occidental Petroleum Software Engineer Interview Questions 2026
...
# Occidental Petroleum Software Engineer interview questions & guide 2026
...
### What is a Software Engineer at Occidental Petroleum?
...
As a **Software Engineer** at **Occidental Petroleum**, you are at the intersection of traditional energy operations and cutting-edge digital transformation. You will contribute to the development and maintenance of critical systems—ranging from **SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition)** support and automation to data-driven platforms that optimize drilling, completions, and asset integrity. Your work directly impacts how **Occidental Petroleum** manages its vast operational footprint, ensuring efficiency, safety, and sustainability in high-stakes environments.
...
The role demands more than just technical proficiency; it requires a deep appreciation for the **operational reality** of the oil and gas industry. You will be expected to bridge the gap between complex software architectures and the physical requirements of field operations. Whether you are building tools for **HSE (Health, Safety, and Environment)** enablement or refining engineering data workflows, your code serves as the backbone for decision-making processes that drive one of the world's most vital industries.
...
Your preparation should focus on demonstrating both high-level system thinking and the ability to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team. At **Occidental Petroleum**, the interviewers are not just looking for a programmer; they are looking for a teammate who can navigate the unique challenges of the energy sector.
...
### Key Responsibilities
...
As a **Software Engineer** at **Occidental Petroleum**, your primary responsibility is to design, develop, and maintain robust software solutions that support the company’s operational assets. You will often work alongside process engineers and asset directors to translate physical operational needs into digital requirements.
...
This role involves significant collaboration. You will not be working in a silo; you will be expected to communicate with stakeholders across various departments, including IT, field operations, and project management. You may be tasked with automating manual data collection, enhancing **SCADA** monitoring systems, or participating in the lifecycle management of applications that ensure the safety and efficiency of drilling and production facilities.
...
### Role Requirements & Qualifications
...
* **Technical Skills**: Proficiency in modern programming languages relevant to your specific track (e.g., Python, C#, or SQL), experience with database management, and familiarity with **SCADA** or industrial control systems is highly advantageous.
...
* **Must-have**: A genuine interest in the energy sector and a
...
to safety and operational excellence.
...
* **Nice-
...
The **Software Engineer** role at **Occidental Petroleum** offers a unique opportunity to apply your technical skills to high-impact, real-world challenges in the energy sector. By focusing on your ability to solve practical problems, demonstrating strong interpersonal skills, and showing a genuine interest in the company’s operations, you will position yourself as a top-tier candidate.
...
## Other roles at Occidental Petroleum
...

results.title: Senior Data Architect - MyPetroCareer
highlights:
Senior Data Architect - MyPetroCareer
...
Halliburton
...
We are looking for the right people — people who want to innovate, achieve, grow and lead. We attract and retain the best talent by investing in our employees and empowering them to develop themselves and their careers. Experience the challenges, rewards and opportunity of working for one of the world’s largest providers of products and services to the global energy industry.
...
The Data Architect provides the technical leadership for Landmark’s AAM/UEM data platform — the layer that ingests, normalizes, and exposes oil & gas data (subsurface, drilling, well planning, production, finance, HSE) for the application layer and, increasingly, for external operators. You will own the data model, the ingestion and harmonization strategy across heterogeneous source systems, and the architectural roadmap for the AI-augmented data workflows that set the platform apart from a generic “ingest everything” data lake.
...
This is a deliberately technical, hands-on architecture role. It pairs day-to-day with an internal Data Platform Product Manager, who owns the “what and why” on the product and customer side, while you own the “how” on the data side. The two roles were created together so that customer outcomes and data architecture move in step rather than in opposition. You will work closely with engineers, data scientists, and domain experts, and your decisions about how the platform models and serves data will shape what the product can credibly promise.
...
AI-Augmented Data Workflows
...
- Own the architecture for AI-augmented data workflows — retrieval-augmented generation, semantic and vector search, and agentic pipelines — that make the platform’s data genuinely useful to engineers and scientists, not just stored.
- Decide where AI belongs in a data platform for upstream oil & gas, separating real, high-value use cases from buzzwords, and build the patterns the rest of the team will follow.
- Stay hands-on enough to prototype and validate these workflows yourself, using modern AI-assisted development tools as part of your daily working practice.
...
- Master’s degree in Computer Science or a closely related STEM discipline.
...
- Extensive experience in data architecture — data modelling, schema design, ingestion patterns, data cataloguing, governance, and event-
...
data flows.
...
- Hands-on experience
...
modern cloud data platforms and tooling (e.g. Azure, Microsoft Fabric, SQL and cloud databases, vector databases, graph databases).
...
- Practical, shipped experience with AI-augmented data workflows — RAG, semantic and vector search, and agentic pipelines — not just evaluation or demos.
...
- Familiarity with oil & gas or energy data domains — subsurface, well, drilling, production, HSE, or finance data.
...
- Comfort with AI-assisted development tools (e.g. Claude Code, Copilot, Cursor, MCP-based workflows) in daily work.
...
- Familiarity with ontology- or meta-model-driven integration approaches.
- Experience with modern agentic AI frameworks (e.g. LangChain, LangGraph, Pydantic-AI) and the patterns for putting them into production.
- Fluent Norwegian
...
and the ability to work cred

highlights:
# Senior AI/ML Engineer
...
We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.
...
Develop AI and ML solutions for
...
energy and utility sector

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems
...

...
Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen
...
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems.
...
- Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development.
...
- Contribute to the design and implementation of modern AI architectures, including RAG systems, LLM integrations, and agent-based workflows.
...
- Develop frameworks for
...
and monitoring AI
...
, including reasoning
...

...
- Stay current with advancements in machine learning engineering, generative AI, and AI orchestration, integrating best practices into workflows.
...
- Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Software Engineering, or related field (PhD preferred).
...
- 7+ years of experience building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning systems in production environments.
...
- Strong proficiency in Python, including experience building reusable packages and scalable data pipelines.
...
- Hands-on experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP, including CI/CD and containerized deployments.
...
- Solid understanding of ML system design, MLOps principles, and end-to-end model lifecycle management.
...
- Experience with distributed computing frameworks and relational/non-relational databases.
...
- Familiarity with Databricks and large-scale data processing environments.
...
- Experience working with generative AI frameworks such as LangChain, LangGraph, LlamaIndex, Hugging Face, or AutoGen.
...
- Exposure to agentic AI systems, AI orchestration, or LLM-based decision workflows is highly desirable.
...
- Nice to have: experience in the utility, energy, or power sector and strong Databricks expertise.

I am a Senior Advisor in AI Engineering (AI Architect) with over 15 years of experience in the oil and gas industry, specializing in designing and building scalable cloud platforms and enterprise AI solutions. My work focuses on architecting and delivering AI-driven systems. I have led initiatives to operationalize predictive models, establish MLOps practices, and develop cloud-native applications that enable organizations to leverage data and machine learning at scale. With a strong foundation in cloud infrastructure across AWS and Azure, I specialize in Kubernetes-based deployments, event-driven architectures, and secure, enterprise-grade platforms. I am passionate about enabling teams to deliver reliable, scalable, and production-ready AI solutions. results.title: Associate CASS Data Scientist | Wfh
highlights:
Associate CASS Data Scientist | Wfh
...
# Associate CASS Data Scientist
...
Posted Jun 18, 2026
...
As an Associate CASS Data Scientist, you’ll help drive the analytical backbone of Southwest’s Continuing Analysis and Surveillance System (CASS), turning complex operational data into insights that strengthen safety, compliance, and reliability. You’ll develop automated workflows, build models, and apply advanced analytics—including machine learning and AI—to detect trends, assess risk, and optimize the health of our Continuous Airworthiness Maintenance Program. Partnering closely with Tech Ops, CASS Leadership, and cross‑functional stakeholders, you’ll create dashboards and predictive tools that inform critical decisions and support the ongoing improvement of our maintenance programs. By blending strong analytical methods with real‑world operational understanding, you’ll play a key role in ensuring our fleet stays safe, efficient, and ready to serve our Customers.
...

life cycle including engaging stakeholders to define the problem, solution definition, project planning, and executing per plan

### Pipeline Control AI Investment
...
Investment in CruxOCM's AI-driven pipeline control technology through $17 million Series A funding
...
ONEOK invested alongside Microsoft Ventures Fund in CruxOCM, a Calgary-based company developing co-pilot technologies for pipeline control room operators. The technology includes pipeBOT, max-OPT and leanOPT focused on automated pipeline control and AI-driven software to improve operational and sustainability efficiencies.

ONEOK is actively pursuing opportunities to provide energy infrastructure for AI data centers across the United States

The company's intrastate assets are strategically located in premier natural gas supply-and-demand centers close to many proposed AI data center projects, providing speed-to-market advantages. ONEOK can meet timing needs with existing infrastructure rather than requiring new construction.

They focus on safely delivering reliable energy for the future. They offer services in midstream, marketing, chemicals, emerging energy, energy research & innovation, and aviation. They also provide safety data sheets, interest owners analysis, and sustainability initiatives. Phillips 66 employs 7,035 people (+4.9% YoY, +621 people) and has an annual revenue of $132.4B, founded in 2012. Headquartered in Houston, Texas, United States, operates in 28 countries (including United Kingdom, Singapore, Canada, Germany, and Ireland). Has a $45.7B market cap. IPO 2012.
